name: Network Call on Main Thread
bad_example: Calling retrofit.execute() or URLSession.dataTask synchronously on the main thread.
why_bad: Blocks the UI thread causing ANR (Android) or frozen UI (iOS). Unresponsive for >5s triggers system kill.
good_example: Use suspend functions with Dispatchers.IO (Android) or async/await (Swift) for all network calls.
keywords: main thread anr network block ui freeze

name: Missing Network Error Handling
bad_example: try { api.getData() } catch(e: Exception) { /* empty */ } — silently swallowing errors.
why_bad: Users see blank screens with no feedback. Impossible to diagnose production issues.
good_example: Show error state UI; implement retry mechanism; log to crash reporting. Use sealed class Result<T>.
keywords: error handling silent catch empty network

name: Hardcoded API URLs in Source Code
bad_example: val BASE_URL = 'https://api.production.com' directly in source file.
why_bad: Cannot switch between dev/staging/prod without rebuilding. Secrets in version control.
good_example: Use BuildConfig fields (Android) or xcconfig (iOS). Inject via build flavors/schemes.
keywords: hardcoded url api secret buildconfig xcconfig

name: No Offline Support
bad_example: Always fetching from network with no local cache or error state for no connectivity.
why_bad: App becomes unusable without internet. Users in poor connectivity areas cannot use core features.
good_example: Implement repository pattern with local-first strategy. Show cached data with staleness indicator.
keywords: offline cache network unavailable local first

name: Leaked Listeners & Callbacks
bad_example: Registering broadcast receiver in onCreate but never unregistering in onDestroy.
why_bad: Memory leak grows with each configuration change. Eventually causes OOM crash.
good_example: Register in onStart/onResume and unregister in onStop/onPause. Use lifecycle-aware components.
keywords: memory leak listener callback register unregister lifecycle

name: God Activity / God ViewController
bad_example: Single Activity with 3000+ lines handling UI; networking; database; and navigation.
why_bad: Untestable; impossible to maintain; violates single responsibility principle.
good_example: Extract into ViewModel + Repository + UseCase. Each class under 300 lines. Apply Clean Architecture.
keywords: god activity viewcontroller monolith srp

name: Missing ProGuard/R8 in Release Build
bad_example: Publishing release APK without code shrinking or obfuscation enabled.
why_bad: Larger APK size; exposed source code; easier to reverse engineer.
good_example: Enable minifyEnabled and shrinkResources in release buildType. Add keep rules for reflection.
keywords: proguard r8 minify shrink obfuscate release

name: Ignoring Activity/Fragment Lifecycle
bad_example: Starting coroutines in Activity without lifecycle scope or cancellation.
why_bad: Coroutines continue after Activity is destroyed causing crashes or wasted resources.
good_example: Use lifecycleScope or viewModelScope. Collect flows in repeatOnLifecycle(STARTED).
keywords: lifecycle coroutine scope cancel activity fragment

name: Missing Accessibility Support
bad_example: ImageButton without contentDescription. Custom view without semantics.
why_bad: Excludes users with disabilities. Fails WCAG compliance. App store rejection risk.
good_example: Add contentDescription to all images. Use Compose semantics. Test with TalkBack/VoiceOver.
keywords: accessibility a11y contentdescription talkback voiceover

name: Storing Secrets in SharedPreferences
bad_example: Saving JWT tokens or passwords in plain-text SharedPreferences/UserDefaults.
why_bad: Readable by any rooted device or backup extraction. Major security vulnerability.
good_example: Android: EncryptedSharedPreferences or DataStore + EncryptedFile. iOS: Keychain Services.
keywords: security storage secrets keychain encrypted preferences

name: No Client-Side Input Validation
bad_example: Accepting any text in form fields without validation before submission.
why_bad: SQL injection via backend; XSS if content is displayed; wasted API calls with invalid data.
good_example: Validate email format; password strength; phone number pattern. Sanitize before display.
keywords: input validation form sanitize xss injection

name: Blocking Animation on Main Thread
bad_example: Running complex animation calculations synchronously on the main thread.
why_bad: Frame drops; jank; poor UX. Especially visible on 120Hz displays.
good_example: Use withFrameNanos (Compose) or CADisplayLink (iOS). Offload heavy math to background.
keywords: animation frame drop jank main thread blocking

name: No Loading State Indicator
bad_example: Fetching data without showing loading indicator to the user.
why_bad: User thinks app is broken or frozen. Taps repeatedly causing duplicate requests.
good_example: Show skeleton screens or progress indicators. Disable action buttons during loading.
keywords: loading state indicator progress skeleton ux

name: Hardcoded Strings in Code
bad_example: Text('Hello World') directly in composable or view code instead of resources.
why_bad: Cannot localize. Cannot update text without code change and recompilation.
good_example: Android: strings.xml resources. iOS: Localizable.strings or String Catalogs. Use R class or NSLocalizedString.
keywords: string hardcoded localization i18n resource

name: Untested Deep Links
bad_example: Adding deep link intent filters but never testing them with adb or xcrun.
why_bad: Users land on crash screens or wrong destinations from notifications or web links.
good_example: Test with: adb shell am start -a android.intent.action.VIEW -d 'scheme://path'. iOS: xcrun simctl openurl.
keywords: deep link test intent filter universal link
references: Android Deep Links; Apple Universal Links

name: Fragment Constructor Arguments
bad_example: Passing data to Fragment via constructor parameters instead of arguments Bundle.
why_bad: Fragment with constructor args crashes on recreation (process death or config change).
good_example: Use Fragment.newInstance() with arguments Bundle or Navigation Safe Args.
keywords: fragment constructor arguments bundle newinstance

name: Using GlobalScope for Coroutines
bad_example: GlobalScope.launch { networkCall() } without structured concurrency.
why_bad: Coroutine cannot be cancelled; leaks if parent scope is destroyed. No structured concurrency.
good_example: Use viewModelScope; lifecycleScope; or custom CoroutineScope with SupervisorJob.
keywords: globalscope coroutine structured concurrency scope leak

name: Force Unwrapping Optionals (Swift)
bad_example: let value = optionalValue! without nil check.
why_bad: Runtime crash (fatal error: unexpectedly found nil) in production.
good_example: Use guard let; if let; or nil-coalescing operator (??) for safe unwrapping.
keywords: force unwrap optional nil crash swift guard

name: Ignoring Dark Mode Support
bad_example: Using hardcoded colors (Color.White) instead of semantic colors.
why_bad: Unreadable text and eye strain in dark mode. Poor UX for 80%+ of users who use dark mode.
good_example: Use theme colors: MaterialTheme.colorScheme (Compose) or Color asset catalog (iOS). Test both modes.
keywords: dark mode theme color hardcoded semantic
references: Material Design 3 Theming; Apple Dark Mode

name: Massive ViewModel (1000+ lines)
bad_example: ViewModel containing network calls; database queries; validation; and UI formatting logic.
why_bad: Untestable; hard to understand; violates SRP. Every change risks breaking unrelated features.
good_example: Extract UseCases for business logic. Keep ViewModel as state holder only. Max 200-300 lines.
keywords: viewmodel massive srp usecase extract

name: Not Cancelling Background Work
bad_example: Starting download or upload without cancellation support when user navigates away.
why_bad: Wasted bandwidth; battery drain; potential crashes from updating destroyed UI.
good_example: Check isActive in long loops. Use withContext(NonCancellable) only for cleanup. Respect cancellation.
keywords: cancel coroutine background work download isactive

name: No Crash Reporting in Production
bad_example: Releasing app without crash reporting integration.
why_bad: Blind to production issues. Cannot prioritize fixes. Users silently churn.
good_example: Integrate Firebase Crashlytics or Sentry. Upload mapping files. Monitor crash-free rate.
keywords: crash reporting crashlytics sentry production monitoring

name: Missing Database Indexes
bad_example: Querying Room/CoreData on columns without indexes on frequently filtered columns.
why_bad: Slow queries (full table scan) on large datasets causing UI lag.
good_example: Add @Index to Room entities. Add indexes in CoreData model editor. Analyze with EXPLAIN QUERY PLAN.
keywords: database index query slow room coredata

name: Not Handling Keyboard in Forms
bad_example: Form fields hidden behind keyboard with no scroll adjustment or keyboard avoidance.
why_bad: Users cannot see what they are typing. Critical fields become inaccessible.
good_example: Android: imePadding() in Compose or windowSoftInputMode=adjustResize. iOS: keyboard avoidance in SwiftUI.
keywords: keyboard form hidden input adjust ime

name: Database Query on Main Thread
bad_example: Room query without suspend or LiveData — runs synchronously blocking UI.
why_bad: ANR for queries taking >16ms. Especially bad for large datasets or complex joins.
good_example: Always use suspend functions or Flow for Room queries. Use allowMainThreadQueries() only in tests.
keywords: database main thread query room anr suspend

name: Redundant Kotlin Plugin in AGP 9.x
bad_example: Adding alias(libs.plugins.kotlin.android) while using AGP 9.1+.
why_bad: AGP 9.x applies Kotlin by default. Adding it again causes 'Cannot add extension with name kotlin' crash.
good_example: Remove org.jetbrains.kotlin.android from build.gradle.kts and libs.versions.toml.
keywords: agp 9 extension already registered kotlin

name: Legacy kotlinOptions in AGP 9.x
bad_example: Using kotlinOptions { jvmTarget = '11' } in AGP 9.x build files.
why_bad: Accessor is removed/deprecated in AGP 9.x due to Built-in Kotlin feature.
good_example: Use kotlin { jvmToolchain(11) } outside the android {} block.
keywords: kotlinoptions jvmtarget unresolved reference agp 9

name: KSP SourceSet Block in AGP 9.x
bad_example: Running KSP without android.disallowKotlinSourceSets flag in AGP 9.x.
why_bad: AGP 9.x blocks the kotlin.sourceSets DSL used by KSP for generated code.
good_example: Add android.disallowKotlinSourceSets=false to gradle.properties.
keywords: ksp sourcesets dsl not allowed agp 9

name: Unregistered Activity in Manifest
bad_example: Adding a new Activity class but missing the <activity> tag in AndroidManifest.xml.
why_bad: Throws ActivityNotFoundException when attempting to start the activity.
good_example: Always register new activities in AndroidManifest.xml with appropriate exported tag.
keywords: activitynotfoundexception manifest unregistered

name: Missing Room Migration
bad_example: Changing Entity schema (adding/removing columns) without incrementing version or adding Migration.
why_bad: App crashes on startup with IllegalStateException: Room cannot verify data integrity.
good_example: Increment @Database version and provide Migration or fallbackToDestructiveMigration().
keywords: room migration version schema change crash

name: Intent Extra Key Mismatch
bad_example: Using different keys for putExtra() and getExtra() (e.g. 'ID' vs 'id').
why_bad: getExtra() returns null or default value; leading to NullPointerException or wrong data.
good_example: Use constant companion object properties for Intent keys to ensure consistency.
keywords: intent extra key mismatch nullpointer

name: RecyclerView Item Match Parent
bad_example: Setting layout_height='match_parent' for a single item in a RecyclerView list.
why_bad: The first item takes up the entire screen; hiding all subsequent items in the list.
good_example: Always set layout_height='wrap_content' for individual list item layouts.
keywords: recyclerview item height match_parent hidden
